name: Supply Chain and Operations ValuePack
description: Vertical-specialized intelligence pack for supply chain and operations functions within manufacturing. Covers procurement, supplier quality, production planning, inventory optimization, warehousing, logistics, MRO, quality assurance, and EHS compliance. Provides signal rules, KPIs, benchmarks, and value formulas specific to flow efficiency, cost optimization, risk mitigation, and operational excellence.

pains.name: Supplier PPM Spike from Geopolitical Disruption
pains.description: Sudden increase in supplier defect rates driven by geopolitical events, trade restrictions, or forced supplier changes disrupting incoming material quality.
symptoms: Quality holds on raw materials increasing

pains.name: MRO Inventory Obsolescence and Stockouts
pains.description: Critical spare parts unavailable during breakdowns while slow-moving MRO inventory accumulates, creating both downtime risk and working capital waste.
symptoms: MRO parts availability <85% for critical assets
MRO write-offs >3% of MRO inventory value
Emergency MRO purchases >20% of MRO spend

pains.name: Warehouse Space Constraints and Poor Slotting
pains.description: Warehouse capacity at or exceeding limits with inefficient slotting causing picking travel time waste, congestion, and inability to accommodate growth.
symptoms: Picking travel time >50% of total order cycle time
Congestion at inbound and outbound docks

pains.name: Ineffective Demand Planning and S&OP
pains.description: Demand forecasts statistically poor with low S&OP process maturity causing mismatches between supply plans and actual demand across the network.
symptoms: S&OP meetings ad-hoc or monthly at best
Sales and supply chain plans disconnected

pains.name: Poor Supplier Scorecard and Performance Visibility
pains.description: Lack of systematic supplier performance measurement preventing proactive issue identification and data-driven sourcing decisions.
symptoms: Supplier scorecards updated quarterly or less
Sourcing decisions based on price only
Supplier quality issues discovered at receiving

pains.name: Cross-Border Logistics Delay and Customs Complexity
pains.description: International shipments experiencing extended transit times, customs holds, and documentation errors disrupting production schedules.
symptoms: No visibility to shipment location in transit

pains.name: Production Planning System unable to Handle Constraints
pains.description: ERP-based planning with infinite capacity assumptions producing unexecutable schedules requiring constant manual intervention.
symptoms: Manual plan adjustments >80% of planning hours
Material and capacity constraints checked separately

pains.name: EHS Compliance Data Fragmentation and Audit Readiness
pains.description: Safety, environmental, and compliance data scattered across paper logs, spreadsheets, and disconnected systems preventing real-time compliance visibility.

pains.name: Last-Mile and Customer Delivery Failures
pains.description: Poor execution in final delivery segment causing customer penalties, chargebacks, and damaged relationships.
symptoms: Customer claim rate >2% on delivery issues
No real-time delivery tracking to customer

pains.name: Safety Stock Misalignment and Service Level Degradation
pains.description: Safety stock policies based on rules of thumb rather than statistical methods causing chronic stockouts on high-velocity items and excess on slow movers.
symptoms: Safety stock days uniform across all SKUs
No ABC/XYZ segmentation for inventory policy

pains.name: Procurement Maverick Spend and Off-Contract Buying
pains.description: Significant portion of indirect and direct spend occurring outside negotiated contracts, eroding negotiated savings and compliance.

pains.name: Inbound Receiving and Put-Away Bottlenecks
pains.description: Delays in receiving dock processing causing trailer detention, delayed inventory availability, and production material shortages.

pains.name: Conflict Minerals and Supply Chain Transparency Gaps
pains.description: Inability to trace material origins down to smelter and refiner level for conflict minerals and ESG reporting requirements.
symptoms: Customer ESG scorecards require full chain
No blockchain or digital traceability platform

pains.name: Freight Cost Inflation and Poor Modal Optimization
pains.description: Rising freight rates combined with suboptimal modal selection and poor load consolidation eroding logistics margins.
symptoms: Modal selection based on habit not optimization
No freight audit and payment system
Spot market exposure >30% of freight spend

pains.name: Quality System Silos from Supplier to Customer
pains.description: Disconnected quality data across incoming inspection, in-process, final inspection, and customer returns preventing end-to-end quality analytics.
symptoms: No single view of quality from supplier to customer
Supplier quality data in different system than internal
Customer complaint root cause takes >30 days
No correlation between supplier PPM and field failures
Quality costs not traceable to source

pains.name: SRM Platform Gaps and Poor Supplier Collaboration
pains.description: Lack of Supplier Relationship Management capabilities preventing electronic PO collaboration, invoice matching, and supplier self-service.
symptoms: No supplier portal for PO/ASN visibility
No electronic request for quote process
Vendor managed inventory limited to top 5% suppliers

pains.name: Backorder Escalation and Customer Penalties
pains.description: Chronic backorders triggering contractual penalties, customer scorecard deductions, and competitive displacement.

pains.name: Cross-Dock Underutilization and Flow-Through Inefficiency
pains.description: Failure to leverage cross-docking and flow-through strategies forcing unnecessary put-away, storage, and re-handling of fast-moving goods.
symptoms: Fast movers stored then picked same day
No appointment scheduling for inbound/outbound synchronization
Storage cost for items with dwell time <4 hours
No real-time visibility of inbound/outbound alignment

kpis.name: Order Fill Rate
kpis.formula: (Orders Shipped Complete / Total Orders) x 100

kpis.name: Carrier On-Time Delivery
kpis.formula: (On-Time Carrier Deliveries / Total Carrier Deliveries) x 100

kpis.name: MRO Parts Availability
kpis.formula: (Critical MRO Parts in Stock / Total Critical MRO Parts) x 100

kpis.name: Warehouse Productivity
kpis.formula: Total Units (or Lines) Picked / Total Warehouse Labor Hours

kpis.name: Safety Stock Coverage
kpis.formula: Current Inventory / Average Daily Demand (during lead time)

kpis.name: Supplier Lead Time Variability
kpis.formula: (Standard Deviation of Actual Lead Times / Mean Lead Time) x 100

kpis.name: Cross-Dock Percentage
kpis.formula: (Cross-Docked Volume / Total Warehouse Volume) x 100

kpis.name: Backorder Rate
kpis.formula: (Backorder Lines / Total Order Lines) x 100

kpis.name: Dock-to-Stock Time
kpis.formula: Sum of (Receipt Available Timestamp - Receipt Creation Timestamp) / Total Receipts

kpis.name: Cube Utilization
kpis.formula: (Actual Cubic Space Used / Total Available Cubic Space) x 100

kpis.name: Supplier Scorecard Composite
kpis.formula: Weighted average of Quality (PPM), Delivery (OTD), Cost (PPV), Responsiveness, and Compliance scores

kpis.name: Perfect Order Rate
kpis.formula: (Orders Complete, Accurate, On-Time, and Damage-Free / Total Orders) x 100

kpis.name: Maverick Spend Ratio
kpis.formula: (Off-Contract Spend / Total Addressable Spend) x 100

kpis.name: Contract Compliance Rate
kpis.formula: (Spend Under Contract / Total Addressable Spend) x 100

kpis.name: Procurement Operating Cost Ratio
kpis.formula: (Total Procurement Operating Cost / Managed Spend) x 100

kpis.name: Receiving Accuracy
kpis.formula: (Receipts with Zero Discrepancy / Total Receipts) x 100

kpis.name: Supply Chain Traceability Coverage
kpis.formula: (Volume with Full Lot/Batch Traceability / Total Volume) x 100

kpis.name: CAPA Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Sum of (CAPA Close Date - CAPA Open Date) / Number of CAPAs Closed

kpis.name: Stockout Rate
kpis.formula: (Stockout Events / Total Demand Events) x 100

kpis.name: Demand Volatility Index
kpis.formula: (Standard Deviation of Weekly Demand / Mean Weekly Demand) x 100

kpis.name: Expediting Cost Ratio
kpis.formula: (Rush Orders + Premium Freight + Overtime for Expediting) / COGS x 100

kpis.name: Inventory Obsolescence Rate
kpis.formula: (Inventory Write-Off / Average Inventory Value) x 100

valueDrivers.name: Supplier Risk and Quality Reduction
valueDrivers.description: Reducing supplier-related quality defects, lead time variability, and supply disruption through scorecards, development, and dual-sourcing
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Defect cost avoidance + customer penalty elimination + reduced inspection + reduced expediting

valueDrivers.name: Logistics and Freight Cost Reduction
valueDrivers.description: Optimizing modal mix, carrier selection, load consolidation, and freight audit to reduce transportation spend
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Modal shift savings + consolidation savings + audit recoveries + detention avoidance

valueDrivers.name: Warehouse Efficiency and Productivity
valueDrivers.description: Improving warehouse labor productivity, space utilization, and order accuracy through slotting, WMS, and automation
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Labor cost reduction + error cost reduction + space deferral + OT reduction

valueDrivers.name: Planning and Scheduling Accuracy Improvement
valueDrivers.description: Improving plan attainment, forecast accuracy, and schedule stability through APS, S&OP, and demand planning
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Throughput increase from stable plans + expediting reduction + overtime reduction + customer retention

valueDrivers.name: MRO Inventory and Availability Optimization
valueDrivers.description: Balancing MRO parts availability for critical assets with inventory reduction through criticality-based stocking policies
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Inventory cash release + downtime avoidance + emergency purchase premium reduction

formulas.name: Safety Stock Optimization Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Baseline Safety Stock Days - Target Safety Stock Days) × (COGS/365) + (Stockout Reduction × Margin per Unit × Stockout Volume) - (Safety Stock Holding Cost × Target Days × WACC)

confidenceRules.condition: Lead time data available for >80% of SKUs

formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 45 days, target 28 days, COGS $100M, stockout 8% to 2%, margin $50/unit, 500 units/day stockout volume, WACC 10%. Working capital release: (45-28) × ($100M/365) = $4.66M. Stockout value: (8%-2%) × $50 × 500 × 365 = $547,500. Holding cost offset: 28 × ($100M/365) × 10% = $767,000. Net value: $4.66M + $547K - $767K = $4.44M/year. Confidence: HIGH with 18 months demand history.

formulas.name: Supplier PPM Reduction Financial Impact
formulas.formulaExpression: (Baseline PPM - Target PPM) / 1,000,000 × Annual Parts Received × (Inspection Cost + Rework Cost + Sorting Cost + Line Stop Cost per Defect) + Avoided Customer Penalties

confidenceRules.condition: Cost per defect validated by finance

formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 2,500 PPM, target 500 PPM, 10M parts/year, $15 inspection + $45 rework + $200 line stop = $260/defect. PPM reduction: 2,000. Defects avoided: 2,000/1M × 10M = 20,000. Value: 20,000 × $260 = $5.2M/year + $800K avoided penalties = $6.0M/year. Confidence: HIGH.

formulas.name: MRO Inventory Optimization Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (MRO Inventory Reduction × MRO Inventory Value) + (Avoided Downtime from Availability Improvement × Hourly Production Value) + (Emergency Purchase Premium Avoided) - (Implementation and Data Cleansing Cost)
formulas.exampleCalculation: MRO inventory $5M, 25% reduction = $1.25M cash release. Availability 82% to 96%, 14% improvement on critical assets. 50 critical assets, 4-hour MTTR, 2 extra breakdowns/year avoided at $50K/hour = $400K. Emergency purchase premium avoided: $5M × 15% × 25% = $188K. Net: $1.25M + $400K + $188K = $1.84M (excluding WACC/time value). Confidence: HIGH with criticality data.

formulas.name: Warehouse Productivity Improvement Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Baseline Units/Hour - Target Units/Hour) / Target Units/Hour × Total Warehouse Labor Hours × Fully Loaded Labor Rate + (Overtime Reduction × OT Premium Rate) + (Accuracy Improvement × Cost per Error)

confidenceRules.condition: WMS data on pick paths and travel time

formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 80 units/hr, target 120 units/hr, 50K labor hours/year, $28/hr loaded. Improvement: (120-80)/120 = 33%. Labor hours saved: 50K × 0.33 = 16,667 hrs = $467K. OT reduction: 5K hrs × $14 premium = $70K. Accuracy 99.2% to 99.7%, 0.5% improvement on 500K orders, $15/error = $37.5K. Total: $574.5K/year. Confidence: HIGH with WMS data.

formulas.name: Freight Cost Reduction via Modal Optimization
formulas.formulaExpression: (Baseline Freight Cost - Optimized Freight Cost) + (Load Consolidation Savings) + (Freight Audit Recoveries) - (TMS Implementation Cost Amortized)

confidenceRules.condition: Freight cost data by mode and lane

formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline freight $12M. Modal optimization (shift 10% from air to ground on suitable lanes): $800K. Load consolidation on 15% of LTL to TL: $600K. Freight audit 2% recovery: $240K. TMS cost: $180K/year. Net: $12M - ($800K + $600K + $240K) = $10.36M. Savings: $1.46M/year. Confidence: HIGH with lane-level data.

formulas.name: Demand Planning Improvement Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Forecast Error Reduction × Demand Variability Cost) + (Inventory Optimization from Better Forecast) + (Obsolescence Reduction) + (Expediting Cost Reduction from Plan Stability) - (Planning System Cost)

confidenceRules.condition: Historical forecast accuracy tracked by SKU

formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline MAPE 42%, target 25%, $200M demand value, 20% carrying cost. Safety stock reduction 15%: $200M × 15% × 20% = $6M. Obsolescence 3% to 1.5%: $200M × 1.5% = $3M. Expediting $2M to $1M = $1M. Total: $10M/year. Confidence: MEDIUM without POS data, HIGH with.

formulas.name: EHS Incident Cost Reduction
formulas.formulaExpression: (TRIR Reduction × Total Labor Hours / 200,000 × (Direct Cost per Incident + Indirect Cost Multiplier × Direct Cost)) + (Insurance Premium Reduction) + (Compliance Admin Cost Reduction) - (EHS Platform Cost)

confidenceRules.condition: Indirect cost multiplier validated by finance

formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline TRIR 4.5, target 2.0, 1M labor hours. Incidents: (4.5-2.0) × 1M/200K = 12.5 incidents avoided. Direct $15K, indirect 4x = $75K/incident. Value: 12.5 × $75K = $937.5K. Insurance reduction 10% on $2M = $200K. Admin reduction 30% on $500K = $150K. Total: $1.29M/year. Confidence: HIGH with complete OSHA data.

formulas.name: Cross-Dock and Flow-Through Savings
formulas.formulaExpression: (Volume Shifted to Cross-Dock × Handling Cost per Touch Avoided) + (Storage Cost Avoided) + (Inventory Carrying Cost Reduction from Reduced Dwell Time) - (Cross-Dock Infrastructure Cost)

confidenceRules.condition: Dwell time by SKU category tracked

formulas.exampleCalculation: Volume 500K pallets/year, shift from 10% to 35% cross-dock = 125K pallets. Handling saved: 2 touches × $8 = $16/pallet = $2.0M. Storage saved: 125K × 3 days × $4 = $1.5M. Carrying cost: 125K × $500 × 15% × (3/365) = $77K. Total: $3.58M/year. Confidence: MEDIUM - requires facility assessment.

formulas.name: Perfect Order Improvement Revenue Protection
formulas.formulaExpression: (Perfect Order Rate Improvement × Annual Order Volume × Average Order Value × Customer Retention Probability × Gross Margin) + (Chargeback and Penalty Avoidance) + (Customer Service Cost Reduction)

confidenceRules.condition: Order accuracy data by error type

formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 88%, target 96%, 100K orders/year, $5K AOV, 30% retention, 25% margin. Improvement value: 8% × 100K × $5K × 30% × 25% = $3.0M. Chargebacks $500K, 50% reduction = $250K. Customer service 20% reduction on $800K = $160K. Total: $3.41M/year. Confidence: HIGH with customer scorecard data.

formulas.name: Maverick Spend Reduction
formulas.formulaExpression: (Maverick Spend Baseline - Maverick Spend Target) × (Average Contract Discount vs Spot Price) + (Procurement Process Cost Reduction from Automated Compliance) + (Supplier Negotiation Leverage from Consolidated Volume)

confidenceRules.condition: Contract coverage and discount data known
Purchase order data with supplier linkage

formulas.exampleCalculation: Addressable spend $80M, maverick 30% to 10% = $16M shift. Avg contract discount 8% = $1.28M. Process cost $400K, 40% reduction = $160K. Volume leverage: $16M × 2% = $320K. Total: $1.76M/year. Confidence: HIGH with categorized spend data.

formulas.name: Production Schedule Attainment Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Schedule Attainment Improvement × Planned Production Hours × Hourly Production Value × Margin Rate) + (Overtime Reduction from Plan Stability) + (Expediting Cost Reduction) - (APS/Planning Software Cost)

confidenceRules.condition: Historical schedule attainment by work center

formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 72%, target 90%, 100K planned hours, $500/hr value, 20% margin. Attainment value: 18% × 100K × $500 × 20% = $1.8M. OT reduction: 8K hrs × $20 = $160K. Expediting $1.2M to $400K = $800K. Total: $2.76M/year. Confidence: HIGH with work center data.

formulas.name: Dock-to-Stock Velocity Improvement
formulas.formulaExpression: (Baseline DTS Hours - Target DTS Hours) / 24 × (Trailer Detention Cost per Day × Trucks per Day) + (Production Material Availability Improvement × Hourly Production Value × Avoided Delay Hours) + (Receiving Labor Cost Reduction)
formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 48 hrs, target 8 hrs, 40 trucks/day, $200 detention/day. DTS improvement: (48-8)/24 × $200 × 40 × 365 = $4.87M. Production availability: 50 hrs avoided × $400/hr × 200 days = $4.0M. Receiving labor: 20% reduction on $1.2M = $240K. Total: $9.11M/year. Confidence: HIGH with WMS timestamp data.

benchmarks.name: MRO Parts Availability - Best in Class
Maverick Spend Ratio - Best in Class
Cross-Dock Percentage - Food/Retail
benchmarks.source: CSCMP / Georgia Tech Supply Chain Study

benchmarks.name: Carrier On-Time Delivery - Best in Class

signalRules.signalName: Supplier PPM spike >3x baseline in quarterly report
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Supplier defect rate escalation reported in quality review or customer scorecard
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Geopolitical supplier shift, alternate source qualification gaps, or incoming inspection capacity constraints are degrading material quality

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Increase in emergency/spot purchase orders for maintenance parts with expedited delivery terms
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: MRO stocking policy misalignment, critical spares stockouts, or asset breakdown frequency increasing

signalRules.signalName: Warehouse space expansion or lease announcement
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: New DC lease, warehouse expansion capex, or third-party warehousing contract award
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Inventory growth outpacing operational efficiency; slotting and cube utilization likely suboptimal

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Cass Freight Index, DAT load-to-truck ratio, or Baltic Dry Index trending sharply upward
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Logistics cost pressure forcing trade-off between service level and margin; modal optimization opportunity urgent

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Job postings for demand planners, S&OP consultants hired, or planning system RFP issued
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Demand-supply mismatch recognized; forecast accuracy and planning cadence inadequate for business complexity

signalRules.signalName: OSHA serious violation or willful citation
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: OSHA enforcement database entry for serious, repeat, or willful violation at facility
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: EHS management system gaps; potential for escalating penalties, insurance impact, and operational disruption

signalRules.signalName: Conflict Minerals or REACH compliance inquiry from customer
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Customer requests CMRT, smelter list, or REACH substance declaration for product line
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Supply chain transparency requirements escalating; lack of traceability infrastructure becoming competitive disadvantage

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Customer portal, scorecard, or 10-K mentions backorders, delivery delays, or OTD degradation
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Inventory policy, demand planning, or supplier execution failure causing revenue risk and relationship damage

signalRules.signalName: Cross-border shipment delay >50% of nominal lead time
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Freight tracking data or customs records showing extended port dwell, customs hold, or rerouting
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Trade policy, customs documentation, or logistics network disruption threatening production continuity

signalRules.signalName: New SRM or procurement platform selection
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: RFP issued for SRM, e-procurement, or supplier collaboration platform; new CPO from tech-forward organization
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Procurement digitization priority; opportunity for integrated supplier quality, scorecard, and collaboration modules

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Job posting surge for warehouse associates, high agency staffing ratio, or labor dispute filings
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Warehouse operational stability at risk; training burden, accuracy degradation, and capacity constraints likely

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Major customer (OEM, retailer) mandates Scope 3 reporting, carbon disclosure, or supplier ESG assessment
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Environmental compliance data and supply chain traceability becoming order qualifiers; non-compliance risks revenue

signalRules.signalName: APS or advanced planning job postings surge
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Multiple job postings for APS consultants, supply chain optimization engineers, or planning architects
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Current planning system recognized as inadequate; finite capacity, constraint-based, or AI-driven planning being evaluated

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: WMS vendor selection, RF scanning upgrade, or warehouse automation pilot announced
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Warehouse technology modernization underway; slotting, labor standards, and dock-to-stock optimization opportunities aligned

signalRules.signalName: Tariff or trade policy change affecting imports
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: New tariff announcements, Section 301/232 actions, or country-of-origin shift in customs data
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Sourcing strategy disruption requiring supplier relocation, qualification, and inventory buffer reconfiguration

signalRules.signalName: Perfect order rate decline >3 consecutive months
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Customer scorecard or internal logistics metrics showing worsening completeness, accuracy, or damage-free rates
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Warehouse execution, carrier performance, or packaging quality degrading; customer penalties and churn risk rising

signalRules.signalName: EHS platform RFP or EHS manager hiring surge
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Job postings for EHS specialists, EHS software RFP, or incident investigation consultant engagement
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: EHS management system gaps driving reactive compliance culture; digital EHS and predictive safety investment likely

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Quarterly financials showing obsolescence charges, slow-moving reserves, or inventory impairment
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Poor demand forecasting, end-of-life management, or inventory policy causing working capital destruction

evidenceSources.name: Cass Freight Index
evidenceSources.description: Monthly index of North American freight shipments and expenditures providing macro logistics cost trends

evidenceSources.name: ISM Manufacturing Report on Business
evidenceSources.description: Monthly PMI and sub-indices including supplier deliveries, prices, and new orders

evidenceSources.name: OSHA Enforcement Data (ITA)
evidenceSources.description: Public database of OSHA inspections, violations, and penalties by establishment

evidenceSources.name: WERC Warehouse Metrics Report
evidenceSources.description: Annual benchmarking study of warehouse operational metrics

evidenceSources.name: Gartner Supply Chain Top 25 / Magic Quadrants
evidenceSources.description: Annual ranking and vendor evaluation for supply chain planning, SRM, and procurement technology

evidenceSources.name: Resilinc Supply Chain Risk Report
evidenceSources.description: Annual and event-based supply chain disruption intelligence

evidenceSources.name: Hackett Group Procurement Benchmark
evidenceSources.description: Comprehensive procurement function benchmarking including maverick spend, cost ratios, and digitization

evidenceSources.name: EPA ECHO Database
evidenceSources.description: Environmental Compliance History Online - facility-level environmental compliance record

buyingTriggers.name: Supplier PPM crisis / customer scorecard red
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Supplier quality failure causing production line stops or customer penalties
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Budget often available from quality or customer penalty avoidance; expedited vendor selection possible

buyingTriggers.name: Major unplanned downtime from MRO stockout
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Critical asset down for >24 hours due to unavailable spare part
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Emergency budget from operations; MRO optimization often bundled with reliability program

buyingTriggers.name: WMS end-of-life or support termination
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Current WMS vendor announces end-of-support or license renewal with >30% price increase
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: RFP process typical; opportunity to bundle slotting, labor management, and WES

buyingTriggers.name: S&OP process maturity assessment failure
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Oliver Wight or external audit finds S&OP at Stage 1 or 2 with material gaps
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Often part of broader supply chain transformation; demand planning and APS typically bundled

buyingTriggers.name: Freight budget overrun >15%
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Logistics costs exceed annual budget due to rate increases, modal mix, or volume surge
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: TMS and freight audit often quick wins with 6-month payback; modal optimization longer cycle

buyingTriggers.name: OSHA serious violation or VPP withdrawal
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: OSHA citation for serious violation or loss of Voluntary Protection Program status
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: EHS platform budget often released from risk reserve or insurance premium savings

buyingTriggers.name: Customer mandates supplier portal or ESG traceability
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Major OEM or retailer requires supplier quality portal, CMRT, or Scope 3 reporting for continued business
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Customer-mandated timelines create urgency; SRM+QMS+traceability often bundled

buyingTriggers.name: Backorder rate triggers customer business review
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Customer convenes business review due to delivery failures; risk of share loss or penalty
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Inventory optimization and demand planning typically fast-tracked; APS may follow

buyingTriggers.name: New warehouse or DC commissioning
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Greenfield warehouse, DC consolidation, or automation initiative creating greenfield system selection
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Greenfield allows best-practice design without legacy constraints; WMS+WES+automation typically bundled

buyingTriggers.name: ERP planning module replacement or upgrade
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: ERP vendor sunset of planning module or company-wide ERP upgrade creating planning evaluation window
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Best-of-breed APS vs ERP-embedded planning is key decision; require APS benchmark proof

buyingTriggers.name: Trade policy change affecting key supplier region
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Tariff imposition, Section 301 action, or trade restriction forces supplier base restructuring
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Sourcing strategy and inventory policy changes; control tower and risk analytics often bundled

buyingTriggers.name: PE acquisition with supply chain diligence findings
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Private equity acquisition with operational due diligence flagging inventory, planning, or logistics as value creation levers
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: 100-day plan often includes SC&Ops quick wins; working capital release prioritized for leverage

buyingTriggers.name: Perfect order rate decline triggers logistics review
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Customer scorecard or internal metric showing 3+ months of perfect order degradation
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Warehouse and carrier performance typically evaluated together; TMS+WMS bundle common

buyingTriggers.name: Inventory write-off >2% triggers working capital initiative
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Board or CFO mandates working capital improvement program after significant obsolescence charge
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Inventory optimization, demand planning, and S&OP typically bundled; working capital release is CFO priority

personas.name: Supplier Quality Engineer
goals: Implement supplier corrective actions with <30-day closure
Achieve supplier PPAP approval on first submission >80%
pressures: Geopolitical supplier changes forcing rapid re-qualification
Customer scorecard penalties for supplier-related defects
Travel budget cuts limiting on-site supplier audits
Supplier quality data scattered across email and spreadsheets
trustedEvidence: AIAG Core Tools (APQP, PPAP, MSA, SPC, FMEA)
dislikedClaims: Vague quality promises without PPAP evidence
ROI claims without defect cost breakdown

personas.name: Supply Chain Planner
personas.role: Production Planning, Scheduling, and Materials Coordination
goals: Reduce planning cycle time to <4 hours
Balance inventory turns with fill rate >97%
pressures: ERP planning modules producing unexecutable plans
Constant firefighting from material shortages
Sales and operations plans disconnected
Frozen period too short to stabilize operations
trustedEvidence: APICS/ASCM CPIM and CSCP body of knowledge
MRP run time and exception report metrics
dislikedClaims: Software promising 100% automation without exception management
ROI without accounting for planner productivity

personas.name: EHS Manager
personas.role: Environmental, Health, and Safety Program Management
goals: Pass all regulatory audits with zero findings
pressures: Regulatory changes (OSHA, EPA, state-level) tracking burden
Incident reporting lagging >24 hours
Permit-to-work and lockout/tagout paper-based
ESG reporting requiring Scope 1/2/3 data
dislikedClaims: ROI focused only on fine avoidance
Generic EHS platforms without industry depth

personas.name: Warehouse Supervisor
personas.role: Warehouse Operations, Receiving, Put-Away, Picking, Shipping
goals: Maintain safety record with zero incidents
pressures: Warehouse space constraints preventing growth
Labor turnover >30% annually
Peak season volume swings >3x baseline
WMS limitations forcing paper-based workarounds
trustedEvidence: Pick path and travel time studies
dislikedClaims: One-size-fits-all automation for all SKU profiles

personas.name: MRO Buyer
goals: Maintain MRO parts availability >95% for critical assets
Reduce MRO spend through catalog and contract compliance
Implement vendor-managed inventory for high-velocity items
pressures: No visibility into maintenance upcoming needs
Thousands of SKUs with low/no usage history
Production pressure to expedite parts at any cost
MRO catalogs fragmented across multiple suppliers
trustedEvidence: Spare parts lead time and usage history
dislikedClaims: MRO as pure cost center to minimize
Savings claims without downtime risk assessment

personas.name: Demand Planner
goals: Shorten planning cycle to weekly rolling
pressures: Sales forecasts override statistical methods without justification
New product launch forecast accuracy poor
Promotion and event impact not modeled
Demand signals (POS, channel) not integrated
trustedEvidence: MAPE and bias trending by product family
dislikedClaims: Software promising to eliminate forecast error

discoveryQuestions.name: Supplier Scorecard Maturity
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How frequently do you update supplier scorecards, and what percentage of your direct spend suppliers have active scorecards with quality, delivery, cost, and responsiveness dimensions?

discoveryQuestions.name: MRO Criticality and Availability
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Do you classify MRO parts by criticality (e.g., ABC based on downtime impact), and what is your current availability rate for critical spare parts when a breakdown occurs?

discoveryQuestions.name: Warehouse Slotting and Cube Utilization
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How do you currently determine product placement in your warehouse, and what is your measured cube utilization versus available capacity?

discoveryQuestions.name: S&OP Process and Forecast Accuracy
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Describe your S&OP cycle: how often does demand, supply, and finance meet to reconcile plans, and what is your SKU-level forecast MAPE with bias?

discoveryQuestions.name: Freight Cost Structure and Modal Mix
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your freight cost as a percentage of revenue, and how do you currently optimize modal selection between parcel, LTL, TL, intermodal, and air?

discoveryQuestions.name: EHS Data Collection and Incident Reporting
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How is safety incident data collected today—paper, spreadsheet, or digital platform—and what is the average lag from incident occurrence to corporate visibility?

discoveryQuestions.name: Dock-to-Stock Process and ASN Integration
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What percentage of your inbound receipts arrive with an advanced shipping notice (ASN/EDI), and what is your measured dock-to-stock time from trailer arrival to system availability?

discoveryQuestions.name: Conflict Minerals and Supply Chain Transparency
discoveryQuestions.questionText: For products containing tin, tantalum, tungsten, or gold, do you have validated smelter and refiner lists back to origin, and what is your CMRT submission completion rate?

discoveryQuestions.name: Backorder Root Cause Analysis
discoveryQuestions.questionText: When backorders occur, what percentage can you trace to a specific root cause—demand forecast error, supplier delay, inventory policy, or warehouse execution?

discoveryQuestions.name: Planning System Constraints and Finite Capacity
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Does your current planning system perform finite capacity scheduling considering material, labor, and tool constraints simultaneously, or does planning require significant manual adjustment after system output?

discoveryQuestions.name: Maverick Spend Visibility
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What percentage of your addressable spend is off-contract or maverick, and how do you detect and redirect this spend to preferred suppliers?

discoveryQuestions.name: Perfect Order Measurement
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Do you measure perfect order rate (complete, accurate, on-time, damage-free), and what is your current rate with breakdown by failure mode?

discoveryQuestions.name: Cross-Border Lead Time Variability
discoveryQuestions.questionText: For your top 10 imported materials, what is the coefficient of variation in actual lead times versus quoted lead times over the past 12 months?

discoveryQuestions.name: Safety Stock Policy by SKU Classification
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Do you apply differentiated safety stock policies by ABC/XYZ classification, or is safety stock calculated uniformly across all SKUs?

discoveryQuestions.name: Carrier Performance and Scorecards
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How do you measure carrier performance, and what percentage of your freight spend is with carriers having active scorecards and contractual service level agreements?

discoveryQuestions.name: Quality Data Silos End-to-End
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Can your quality team trace a field failure back to the specific supplier lot, incoming inspection result, and in-process parameter in a single query?

discoveryQuestions.name: EHS Permit-to-Work Digitalization
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Is your permit-to-work, lockout/tagout, and hot work permit process digitized with real-time status visibility, or is it paper-based with physical routing?

discoveryQuestions.name: SRM Portal and Supplier Collaboration
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What percentage of your direct material suppliers use a supplier portal for purchase order visibility, ASN submission, invoice matching, and quality document exchange?

objections.name: Our suppliers manage their own quality—we cannot control them
objections.description: Prospect believes supplier quality is entirely outside their control and external quality programs are ineffective.
objections.responseStrategy: Reframe as supplier development partnership: share AIAG/Stage-2 PPAP data showing 60-70% of field failures trace to supplier process variation. Offer pilot with top 3 suppliers, measuring PPM and SCAR closure. Position as customer-mandated requirement (IATF 16949 Section 8.4).

objections.name: We already have an ERP planning module—why do we need APS?
objections.description: Prospect views ERP planning as sufficient and questions need for advanced planning.
objections.responseStrategy: Differentiate infinite vs finite capacity: cite Oliver Wight data showing plan attainment <75% with ERP-only. Quantify expediting cost, overtime, and missed delivery from unexecutable plans. Offer pilot on one constrained work center with schedule attainment as metric.

objections.name: Our warehouse is too small to justify a WMS upgrade
objections.description: Prospect assumes WMS only valuable for large warehouses and that current paper/Excel processes are adequate.
objections.responseStrategy: Pivot to cost per error and labor productivity: even a 50K sq ft warehouse at $28/hr with 20% travel time waste can save $200K+ annually. Cloud WMS has subscription pricing. Offer slotting optimization pilot with 30-day productivity measurement.

objections.name: We tried safety stock optimization before and it caused stockouts
objections.description: Previous inventory optimization project failed due to poor data or implementation, creating organizational resistance.
objections.responseStrategy: Diagnose prior failure root cause: usually lack of ABC/XYZ segmentation or ignoring lead time variability. Propose phased approach starting with A-class items only, holding B/C constant. Guarantee service level floor with financial penalty if fill rate drops below agreed threshold.

objections.name: Freight costs are market-driven—we cannot influence them
objections.description: Prospect views freight as purely external cost with no internal leverage.
objections.responseStrategy: Decompose into controllable elements: modal optimization (10-15% savings), load consolidation (5-10%), freight audit recovery (2-3%), and contract compliance (5-8%). Show benchmark companies achieving 20-30% lower freight as % of revenue. Start with freight audit to prove concept.

objections.name: EHS is about culture, not software
objections.description: Prospect believes digital tools cannot improve safety outcomes and that culture is the only lever.
objections.responseStrategy: Agree culture is primary, then show how technology enables culture: real-time near-miss reporting increases reporting 3-5x; predictive analytics on leading indicators prevents incidents; mobile permit-to-work reduces procedural violations by 40%. Position as culture amplifier, not replacement.

objections.name: Our data quality is too poor for advanced analytics
objections.description: Prospect cites data cleanliness as barrier to any supply chain optimization investment.
objections.responseStrategy: Acknowledge and propose data readiness assessment as first paid engagement. Many SC&Ops platforms include data quality modules. Cite case where 80% data accuracy was sufficient for safety stock optimization with A-class items. Offer data cleansing services bundled with implementation.

objections.name: Our customers do not require traceability yet
objections.description: Prospect views traceability as unnecessary cost since direct customers are not currently demanding it.
objections.responseStrategy: Anticipate regulatory and competitive pressure: UFLPA, EU CBAM, and customer ESG scorecards are expanding traceability requirements. Cost of reactive implementation during crisis (recall, detention) is 5-10x cost of proactive build. Offer compliance gap assessment with regulatory roadmap.

objections.name: MRO is too complex with thousands of SKUs—optimization is impossible
objections.description: Prospect overwhelmed by MRO SKU count and variability, assuming optimization not feasible.
objections.responseStrategy: Segment and conquer: apply Pareto (20% of SKUs cause 80% of downtime risk) and criticality matrix. Start with top 50 critical spares only. Show MRO availability improvement from 80% to 95% on critical items with 15-25% inventory reduction on non-critical. Offer asset criticality assessment.

technologySystems.name: Advanced Planning and Scheduling (APS)
technologySystems.description: Finite-capacity, constraint-based production planning and scheduling systems that optimize material and capacity simultaneously

technologySystems.name: Warehouse Management System (WMS)
technologySystems.description: Real-time warehouse operations management covering receiving, put-away, slotting, picking, packing, and shipping

technologySystems.name: Transportation Management System (TMS)
technologySystems.description: Freight planning, execution, audit, and settlement platform with carrier management and modal optimization

technologySystems.name: Supplier Relationship Management (SRM)
technologySystems.description: Supplier lifecycle management, scorecarding, collaboration portal, and electronic sourcing/quotation platform

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Asset Management / CMMS
technologySystems.description: Asset registry, preventive maintenance scheduling, work order management, and MRO inventory optimization

technologySystems.name: Inventory Optimization Platform
technologySystems.description: Statistical safety stock optimization, multi-echelon inventory planning, and service-level driven inventory policy

technologySystems.name: Quality Management System (QMS) - Supplier Module
technologySystems.description: Supplier quality data collection, PPAP/APQP workflow, SCAR management, and incoming inspection integration

technologySystems.name: EHS Management Platform
technologySystems.description: Incident reporting, permit-to-work, environmental monitoring, audit management, and regulatory compliance tracking
typicalVendors: SAP Environment, Health, and Safety Management

technologySystems.name: Supply Chain Control Tower
technologySystems.description: Multi-tier supply chain visibility, disruption sensing, scenario planning, and orchestration across network

technologySystems.name: Digital Freight Marketplace / 4PL Platform
technologySystems.description: Digital freight matching, spot market access, load consolidation, and freight audit/payment automation

technologySystems.name: Demand Planning and S&OP Platform
technologySystems.description: Statistical forecasting, consensus planning, promotion/event modeling, and S&OP workflow management

technologySystems.name: Slotting Optimization Software
technologySystems.description: Algorithmic warehouse slotting based on velocity, cube, affinity, and ergonomics to minimize travel time

technologySystems.name: Track and Trace / Serialization Platform
technologySystems.description: Lot/batch/serial-level product genealogy from raw material to customer with regulatory reporting

technologySystems.name: Warehouse Execution System (WES) / Robotics Orchestration
technologySystems.description: Real-time orchestration of automated storage, retrieval, AGV/AMR fleets, and human-robot collaboration

regulatoryFactors.name: Conflict Minerals (Dodd-Frank Section 1502 / EU Conflict Minerals Regulation)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Public companies and EU importers using tin, tantalum, tungsten, gold (3TG)
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Annual SEC filing (May 31) / EU due diligence ongoing
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: SEC enforcement, customer exclusion, reputational damage

regulatoryFactors.name: EU REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Manufacturers and importers of substances in EU >1 tonne/year
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Ongoing registration phases; SVHC list updated biannually
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Market exclusion, fines up to EUR 50,000+, criminal liability

regulatoryFactors.name: California Proposition 65
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Products sold in California containing listed chemicals
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Ongoing; 60-day notice prior to enforcement action
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $2,500+/day per violation; citizen suit exposure; labeling requirements

regulatoryFactors.name: ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Organizations seeking environmental certification or customer-mandated compliance
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Certification loss, customer audit findings, contract ineligibility

regulatoryFactors.name: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Organizations certifying or maintaining OH&S management systems
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Certification cycle 3 years; transition from OHSAS 18001 complete
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Certification loss, OSHA alignment gaps, insurance premium impact

regulatoryFactors.name: UFLPA (Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Importers of goods from China, especially Xinjiang-origin materials
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: CBP detention, seizure, forfeiture; supply chain disruption; customer penalties

regulatoryFactors.name: EU CBAM (Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Importers of cement, iron/steel, aluminum, fertilizers, electricity, hydrogen to EU
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Carbon border tariff, market exclusion, competitive cost disadvantage

regulatoryFactors.name: FDA FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program
regulatoryFactors.applicability: US importers of food; food facility registration and verification
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Import refusal, facility shutdown, criminal liability, $1,000-$500,000 fines

regulatoryFactors.name: IATF 16949:2016 Supplier Development Requirements
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: OEM exclusion, new business hold, scorecard penalties

regulatoryFactors.name: EPA TSCA Section 5 / Lautenberg Act
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Chemical manufacturers and importers of new chemical substances
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Pre-manufacture notice (PMN) 90 days prior to production
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $50,000/day; civil and criminal liability; production halt

competitorFactors.description: SAP, Oracle, and Infor leverage ERP relationships to position embedded planning modules as 'good enough' against best-of-breed APS
competitorFactors.impact: Pricing pressure on APS vendors; customer tendency to extend ERP rather than evaluate alternatives

competitorFactors.description: AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud building warehouse and logistics vertical solutions competing with traditional WMS vendors
competitorFactors.impact: Commoditization of basic WMS functions; differentiation shifts to optimization and automation orchestration

competitorFactors.description: Digital freight brokers (Uber Freight, Convoy, Transporeon) reducing need for traditional TMS in spot market management
competitorFactors.impact: TMS vendors must integrate with marketplaces or be bypassed for spot freight; contract freight remains TMS domain

competitorFactors.description: Specialized QMS vendors (MasterControl for pharma, ETQ for automotive, IQVIA for life sciences) command premium through regulatory depth
competitorFactors.impact: Generalist quality platforms struggle in regulated segments; vertical compliance expertise is barrier to entry

competitorFactors.description: Amazon Business, Grainger, and MSC Industrial offering VMI and marketplace models reducing need for internal MRO optimization
competitorFactors.impact: MRO optimization must integrate with marketplace catalogs; pure internal catalog consolidation less compelling

competitorFactors.description: o9, Kinaxis, and Blue Yonder expanding from planning into control tower and execution, creating full-suite competition
competitorFactors.impact: Point solutions for visibility or inventory optimization face bundling pressure; best-of-breed must prove superior algorithmic value

workedExamples.name: Automotive Supplier PPM Reduction and Working Capital Release
workedExamples.scenario: Tier-1 automotive seating manufacturer with $400M revenue experiencing supplier PPM spike from 800 to 3,200 after forced supplier relocation due to geopolitical risk. Customer scorecard penalties at $1.2M/year. Incoming inspection backlog of 12 days. MRO availability at 78% causing 4 unplanned line stoppages/month.
workedExamples.solutionApproach: Integrated supplier quality portal with real-time PPM dashboards, automated SCAR workflow, and supplier self-service PPAP submission. Parallel MRO criticality classification and VMI program for top 20% critical spares.

calculations.result: $35.1M/year defect cost avoidance (validated at $8.2M direct after triage)

calculations.formula: Scorecard improvement from red to green band
Availability 78% to 95% + inventory 25% reduction on $8M MRO

workedExamples.caveats: Requires supplier adoption of portal; assumes 70% supplier participation. MRO criticality assessment is 4-week prerequisite.

workedExamples.name: Food Manufacturer Warehouse Productivity and Cross-Dock Flow-Through
workedExamples.scenario: $250M refrigerated food manufacturer with 120K sq ft DC. Dock-to-stock time 36 hours due to manual receiving. Cross-dock <5% despite 40% of volume being fast-moving with pre-sold orders. Warehouse productivity 65 cases/hour. Perfect order rate 91% with 6% damage rate.
workedExamples.solutionApproach: WMS upgrade with RF scanning, ASN/EDI integration, and appointment scheduling. Slotting optimization for velocity-based placement. Cross-dock lane creation for pre-sold fast movers. Packaging redesign for cold-chain durability.

calculations.formula: Cross-dock 5% to 35% on 500K cases: 150K cases × 2 touches × $6 + storage

workedExamples.caveats: Cold-chain WMS requires temperature integration. Cross-dock requires inbound/outbound appointment synchronization. Implementation during peak season (Q4) not recommended.

workedExamples.name: Chemical Manufacturer EHS Compliance and Safety Incident Reduction
workedExamples.scenario: $600M specialty chemical manufacturer with 8 US facilities. TRIR at 4.2 (industry avg 2.8). OSHA recordable incidents 42/year. Workers comp $3.2M/year and rising. EHS data in 12 disconnected spreadsheets. Audit prep takes 3 weeks per facility. EPA reporting errors in past 2 years.
workedExamples.solutionApproach: Unified EHS platform with mobile incident reporting, digital permit-to-work, environmental sensor integration, and automated regulatory reporting. Predictive safety analytics on leading indicators.

calculations.formula: Audit prep 3 weeks to 3 days × 8 audits × $8K/week

workedExamples.caveats: TRIR reduction assumes frontline supervisor engagement and leading indicator KPIs deployed. EHS platform alone will not change culture; change management critical.

createdComponents: 6 new personas (Supply Chain Planner, Supplier Quality Engineer, EHS Manager, Warehouse Supervisor, MRO Buyer, Demand Planner)

overriddenComponents.overrideReason: Added 6 SC&Ops-specific personas (pers-sqe, pers-scm, pers-ehs-mgr, pers-whs, pers-mro, pers-dp) that extend but do not replace master personas. Master personas pers-sc, pers-plan, pers-quality, pers-ehs, pers-logistics remain active and are supplemented.
SC&Ops subpack elevates inventory turns, fill rate, carrier OTD, and MRO availability to primary KPI status vs secondary in master. Reflects functional priority for this vertical.
Supply chain signal rules use higher confidence for geopolitical and logistics signals (0.80-0.95) vs general manufacturing (0.65-0.85) due to availability of real-time freight and customs data.

verticalPersonaAdditions: pers-sqe: Supplier Quality Engineer — front-line quality at supplier interface
pers-scm: Supply Chain Planner — tactical planning, scheduling, and constraint management
pers-ehs-mgr: EHS Manager — site-level compliance and safety programs
pers-whs: Warehouse Supervisor — DC/warehouse operations and fulfillment
pers-mro: MRO Buyer — maintenance, repair, and operations procurement
pers-dp: Demand Planner — statistical forecasting and consensus planning
verticalKPIExtensions: kpi-fill-rate: Order Fill Rate — warehouse/customer-facing inventory availability
kpi-carrier-otd: Carrier On-Time Delivery — logistics execution performance
kpi-mro-availability: MRO Parts Availability — critical spare parts stock levels
kpi-whs-productivity: Warehouse Productivity — units or lines per labor hour
kpi-safety-stock-coverage: Safety Stock Coverage — statistical protection against variability
kpi-supplier-lead-time-var: Supplier Lead Time Variability — coefficient of variation of lead times
kpi-cross-dock-pct: Cross-Dock Percentage — flow-through vs storage inventory
kpi-backorder-rate: Backorder Rate — unfulfilled customer orders due to stockout
kpi-dock-to-stock: Dock-to-Stock Time — receiving velocity metric
kpi-cube-utilization: Cube Utilization — warehouse space efficiency
kpi-supplier-scorecard: Supplier Scorecard Composite — weighted multi-factor supplier rating
kpi-perfect-order: Perfect Order Rate — complete, accurate, on-time, undamaged delivery
